clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall provide educational services described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] for [COURSE_NAME].
The course shall take place on [COURSE_DATES] according to [COURSE_SCHEDULE_REFERENCE].
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.

Where the Services include recording of course sessions, the Service Provider may record and provide access to the recordings only for [RECORDING_USE], and the Client shall obtain any participant consents required for that use.

Where [SUPPORT_PERIOD_INCLUDED] indicates inclusion, the Service Provider shall provide post-course support for [SUPPORT_PERIOD_DURATION] following the course for an additional fee of [SUPPORT_PERIOD_FEE].

Where the Service Provider issues a certificate, it shall be a certificate of completion only and shall not be represented as an accredited qualification unless [ACCREDITATION_STATUS] expressly states otherwise.
Where the Services include access to an online community, the Client and approved participants may access [COMMUNITY_PLATFORM] during [COMMUNITY_ACCESS_PERIOD].
Materials requested beyond those described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] shall be charged at [ADDITIONAL_MATERIAL_FEE] per material set.
The curriculum for the course shall be set out in [CURRICULUM_REFERENCE].

If the Client cancels the course more than [CANCELLATION_NOTICE_PERIOD_DAYS] days before [COURSE_DATES], the Client shall forfeit [CANCELLATION_FEE_PERCENT] of [TOTAL_VALUE]. If the Client cancels within [CANCELLATION_NOTICE_PERIOD_DAYS] days of [COURSE_DATES], the Client shall forfeit the full amount of [TOTAL_VALUE].
The Student shall attend sessions, complete assigned exercises, and comply with any conduct rules communicated by the Service Provider.
The Service Provider shall deliver the course with reasonable skill and care and shall make reasonable efforts to provide the course content according to the published schedule.

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.

The Service Provider retains ownership of the course content, curriculum, slides, and recorded materials. Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Client receives a license to use the materials for [PERMITTED_USE].

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — Certificates Clause explicitly distinguishes completion certificates from accredited qualifications, closing Step 2 risk #1 within the operative clause itself. Duplication: Student Responsibilities vs. Instructor Responsibilities kept distinct and complementary, allocating conduct/participation on one side and teaching/delivery on the other. Loophole closed: Course Recording requires participant consents for the specified use, preventing unauthorized recording distribution. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Intellectual Property explicitly covers recorded materials as well as slides and curriculum, reflecting the full content stack. Complexity: kept Support Period and Community Access as separate optional services because they are genuinely different post-course benefits. Enforceability: cancellation and certificate-status fields are explicit, with completion-only wording preventing implied credentialing. Attack: A student claims the completion certificate equals an accredited credential — mitigated by the Certificates Clause's explicit distinction. Attack: Course recordings are shared broadly without participant consent — mitigated by Course Recording's permission-and-consent requirement. Attack: Client expects extra handouts and worksheets beyond the course fee — mitigated by Additional Materials pricing. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
